model.label: Grok 4.2
model.description: xAI • Large reasoning model designed for real-time information access, coding, and general problem solving.

pick.rationale: Iowa State enters as the No. 2 seed with elite defensive metrics from a demanding Big 12 schedule that align with top-10 projections, creating a quality edge over Virginia's solid top-15 rankings in both KenPom and Torvik despite the Cavaliers' impressive 29-4 record and elite defense. Virginia advanced with a narrow metrics advantage over Tennessee but faces a tougher test against Iowa State's stifling style and tournament experience, though their own top-15 defense offers a credible upset path if they can force turnovers and slow the pace. No major injuries were flagged in recent reports for either side, but the seeding, conference strength, and matchup specifics tilt toward the Cyclones in this neutral-site Sweet 16 contest.

reasoningStep.title: Team Quality and Injury Check
reasoningStep.summary: Iowa State holds the edge as higher seed with presumed superior efficiency from tougher schedule; Virginia's defense provides upset potential but metrics and priors favor Cyclones.
evidence: Virginia at KenPom 13/Torvik 14 with 29-4 record and strong adjDE per lookup
Iowa State described as No. 2 seed with elite Big 12 defense in multiple prior picks and web previews
No significant injuries reported for either team in ESPN and web searches
Prior r32 picks show Iowa State advancing with 71 confidence vs Virginia's 62

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: demo-2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: s16-midwest-2
Current game label: Midwest Sweet 16 2
Round: Sweet 16
Resolved teams: Virginia (virginia) vs Iowa State (iowa-state)
Prior committed picks: 59
